# technitium_dhcp_reserved_lease (Resource)

Manages a single MAC-to-IP reservation in a Technitium DHCP scope. Do not combine with inline reserved_leases on the same technitium_dhcp_scope — the two would fight over the same server-side list.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "technitium_dhcp_scope" "lan" {
  name             = "lan"
  starting_address = "192.168.1.50"
  ending_address   = "192.168.1.250"
  subnet_mask      = "255.255.255.0"
}

resource "technitium_dhcp_reserved_lease" "nas" {
  scope            = technitium_dhcp_scope.lan.name
  hardware_address = "00-11-22-33-44-66"
  ip_address       = "192.168.1.110"
  host_name        = "nas"
  comments         = "storage box"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `hardware_address` (String) Client MAC address (e.g. 00-11-22-33-44-55).
- `ip_address` (String) Reserved IP address inside the scope range.
- `scope` (String) Name of the DHCP scope holding the reservation.

### Optional

- `comments` (String) Free-form comments for the reservation.
- `host_name` (String) Host name override for the client.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Reservation identifier (scope::hardware_address composite).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Reserved leases are imported as <scope>::<hardware address>.
terraform import technitium_dhcp_reserved_lease.nas "lan::00-11-22-33-44-66"
```
